meta.name: Rosenberg Self-Esteem Scale
meta.description: The Rosenberg Self-Esteem Scale (RSES) is the most widely used measure of self-esteem, consisting of 10 items that assess an individual's overall feelings of self-worth and self-acceptance. The scale includes both positively and negatively worded items.
meta.author: Rosenberg (1965)
meta.reference: Rosenberg M. Society and the Adolescent Self-Image. Princeton, NJ: Princeton University Press; 1965.
meta.instruction: Below is a list of statements dealing with your general feelings about yourself. Please indicate how strongly you agree or disagree with each statement.

questions.text: I feel that I am a person of worth, at least on an equal plane with others
I feel that I have a number of good qualities
All in all, I am inclined to feel that I am a failure
I am able to do things as well as most other people
I feel I do not have much to be proud of
I take a positive attitude toward myself
On the whole, I am satisfied with myself
I wish I could have more respect for myself
I certainly feel useless at times
At times I think I am no good at all

ranges.label: Low Self-Esteem
ranges.description: Your self-esteem level is low. You may hold a negative view of your self-worth and tend toward self-criticism. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Try recording your daily positive achievements and strengths
Reduce negative comparisons with others
Practice self-affirmation and positive self-talk
Consider consulting a counselor for self-acceptance training
Read self-growth books and cultivate a growth mindset

ranges.label: Below Average
ranges.description: Your self-esteem level is in the low-average range. You may experience self-doubt in certain situations. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Acknowledge and affirm your achievements and strengths
Set reasonable self-expectations
Practice self-compassion and treat yourself kindly
Develop hobbies that give you a sense of accomplishment
Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise

ranges.label: Average
ranges.description: Your self-esteem level is within the normal range, with a reasonably objective self-perception and moderate self-affirmation. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy self-awareness
Trust in your abilities when facing challenges
Stay open-minded and accept constructive feedback
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences

ranges.label: High Self-Esteem
ranges.description: Your self-esteem level is high, with a positive self-evaluation and strong confidence. You are well-equipped to handle life's challenges. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a positive self-perception
Stay humble and considerate of others' feelings
Use your confidence to inspire and help those around you
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
